ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon-style webshell drop on Exchange (w3wp -> .aspx in OWA/ECP virtual paths)
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's signature Exchange Server compromise: post-CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 (and the 2022 ProxyNotShell / 2023 OWASSRF re-runs) the IIS worker process w3wp.exe, running under an Exchange app-pool identity, drops an .aspx/.asmx webshell (China Chopper, Awen, BlueShellCS, ASPXSpy, BadPotato variants) into a publicly reachable virtual directory. This pair (Exchange app-pool w3wp writing aspx into OWA/ECP/HttpProxy paths) is the single most reliable artefact of HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon initial access on-prem.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\ecp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\*") (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange app-pool w3wp.exe writing .aspx webshell to public Exchange virtual directories
let ExchangeAppPools = dynamic(["MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool","MSExchangeRPCProxyAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool","MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool","MSExchangePowerShellAppPool"]);
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",@"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",@"\ClientAccess\ecp\",@"\V15\FrontEnd\",@"\Exchange Server\V15\"]);
let ShellExt = dynamic([".aspx",".asmx",".ashx",".asp"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (ExchangeAppPools)         // running as an Exchange app pool
| where FolderPath has_any (ExchangePaths)                              // dropped under an Exchange-reachable virtual dir
| where FileName has_any (ShellExt)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"                      // exclude scheduled/system patching
   or InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel =~ "System"                       // BUT keep SYSTEM-level w3wp drops (HAFNIUM runs as the pool identity, often SYSTEM)
| extend SuspiciousNameHint = iff(FileName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{6,12}\.aspx?$" or FileName has_any ("errorEE","errorEEE","errorcheck","OutlookEN","RedirSuiteServerProxy","Microsoft","shellex","help","aspnet_client"), "yes", "no")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain,
          AppPool = extract(@"(MSExchange[A-Za-z]+AppPool)", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, MD5, SuspiciousNameHint,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ReportId
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange app-pool w3wp.exe writing .aspx webshell (ASIM file events)
let ExchangeAppPools = dynamic(["MSExchangeOWAAppPool","MSExchangeECPAppPool","MSExchangeServicesAppPool","MSExchangeRPCProxyAppPool","MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool","MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool","MSExchangePowerShellAppPool"]);
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\",@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",@"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\",@"\ClientAccess\ecp\",@"\V15\FrontEnd\",@"\Exchange Server\V15\"]);
let ShellExt = dynamic([".aspx",".asmx",".ashx",".asp"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where ActingProcessName has "w3wp.exe"
| where ActingProcessCommandLine has_any (ExchangeAppPools)
| where TargetFilePath has_any (ExchangePaths)
| where TargetFileName has_any (ShellExt)
| extend AppPool = extract(@"(MSExchange[A-Za-z]+AppPool)", 1, ActingProcessCommandLine),
         SuspiciousNameHint = iff(TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{6,12}\.aspx?$" or TargetFileName has_any ("errorEE","errorEEE","errorcheck","OutlookEN","RedirSuiteServerProxy","Microsoft","shellex","help"), "yes", "no")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, AppPool,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256, TargetFileMD5,
          SuspiciousNameHint, ActingProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM Exchange Webshell Drop by w3wp.exe
id: 5b1f3a2c-8e4d-4f6b-9a1c-7d2e3f4a5b6c
status: experimental
description: Detects Exchange app-pool w3wp.exe writing .aspx/.asmx webshells into OWA/ECP/HttpProxy virtual directories — Silk Typhoon's hallmark post-ProxyLogon/ProxyShell/OWASSRF drop.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2025/03/05/silk-typhoon-targeting-it-supply-chain/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MSExchangeOWAAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeECPAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeServicesAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeRPCProxyAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeAutodiscoverAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangeMapiFrontEndAppPool'
      - 'MSExchangePowerShellAppPool'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
      - '\ClientAccess\ecp\'
      - '\V15\FrontEnd\'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.asmx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asp'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Exchange Cumulative Update / Security Update installation (will be msiexec/setup.exe child of w3wp, very rare)
  - Legitimate OWA customisation deployments by Exchange admins
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is the technique combination Microsoft and CISA explicitly attribute to HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon (G0125): exploitation of internet-facing Exchange (ProxyLogon CVE-2021-26855 chain, ProxyShell, OWASSRF) followed by an .aspx webshell written by the Exchange app-pool worker. The narrowness of (a) w3wp.exe as initiator AND (b) Exchange app-pool identity in command line AND (c) drop path inside an E

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) post-exploit Exchange recon: w3wp spawning cmd/powershell for AD enumeration & credential dump
ucs.description: Catches the Silk Typhoon post-webshell hands-on-keyboard burst on a compromised Exchange server: the IIS worker (w3wp.exe) spawns cmd.exe/powershell.exe and runs HAFNIUM's signature recon set — net group "Domain Admins" /domain, nltest /dclist, ipconfig /all, procdump on lsass, and 7z/Rar archiving of the offline NTDS / Exchange offline address book. Microsoft's HAFNIUM playbook documents this exact chain after webshell installation.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_name) as children dc(Processes.process_name) as child_variety from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="net.exe" OR Processes.process_name="net1.exe" OR Processes.process_name="nltest.exe" OR Processes.process_name="ipconfig.exe" OR Processes.process_name="whoami.exe" OR Processes.process_name="systeminfo.exe" OR Processes.process_name="tasklist.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump64.exe" OR Processes.process_name="7z.exe" OR Processes.process_name="rar.exe" OR Processes.process_name="makecab.exe" OR Processes.process_name="esentutl.exe" OR Processes.process_name="ntdsutil.exe") (Processes.process="*Domain Admins*" OR Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*ntds.dit*" OR Processes.process="*nltest*dclist*" OR Processes.process="*Get-OabVirtualDirectory*" OR Processes.process="*Set-OabVirtualDirectory*" OR Processes.process="*ExternalUrl*" OR Processes.process="*-ma lsass*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where child_variety>=2 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | rename host as dest
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe (Exchange) spawning recon / credential-dump / archive tools
let HafniumChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe","makecab.exe","esentutl.exe","ntdsutil.exe","reg.exe"]);
let HafniumKeywords = dynamic([
    "Domain Admins","Enterprise Admins","Schema Admins",
    "nltest /dclist","nltest /domain_trusts",
    "ntds.dit","NTDS\\","\\System32\\config\\SYSTEM",
    "-ma lsass","-ma 4"," lsass.exe","sekurlsa","comsvcs.dll",
    "Get-OabVirtualDirectory","Set-OabVirtualDirectory","ExternalUrl",
    "New-MailboxExportRequest","-FilePath \\\\",
    "esentutl.exe /y","vssadmin create shadow"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "MSExchange"                   // restrict to Exchange app-pool w3wp only
| where FileName in~ (HafniumChildren)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (HafniumKeywords)
| summarize 
    EventCount      = count(),
    DistinctChildren = dcount(FileName),
    Children        = make_set(FileName, 32),
    SampleCmds      = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 16),
    FirstSeen       = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen        = max(Timestamp),
    AppPool         = any(extract(@"(MSExchange[A-Za-z]+AppPool)", 1, InitiatingProcessCommandLine))
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where DistinctChildren >= 2                                           // recon burst, not a single benign call
   or SampleCmds has_any (HafniumKeywords)                              // OR a single high-fidelity keyword
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — Exchange w3wp spawning recon / credential-dump / archive tools (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
let HafniumChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe","rar.exe","winrar.exe","makecab.exe","esentutl.exe","ntdsutil.exe","reg.exe"]);
let HafniumKeywords = dynamic([
    "Domain Admins","Enterprise Admins","Schema Admins",
    "nltest /dclist","nltest /domain_trusts",
    "ntds.dit","\\System32\\config\\SYSTEM",
    "-ma lsass","-ma 4"," lsass.exe","sekurlsa","comsvcs.dll",
    "Get-OabVirtualDirectory","Set-OabVirtualDirectory","ExternalUrl",
    "New-MailboxExportRequest",
    "esentutl.exe /y","vssadmin create shadow"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in (HafniumChildren) or CommandLine has_any (HafniumKeywords)
| summarize 
    EventCount       = count(),
    DistinctChildren = dcount(ChildName),
    Children         = make_set(ChildName, 32),
    SampleCmds       = make_set(CommandLine, 16),
    FirstSeen        = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen         = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, Account
| where DistinctChildren >= 2 or SampleCmds has_any (HafniumKeywords)
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Exchange's w3wp.exe should NEVER, in normal operation, spawn cmd.exe, powershell.exe, net.exe, nltest.exe, procdump.exe, 7z.exe or ntdsutil.exe — these only appear when an attacker is interacting with the host through a webshell. The keyword set (Get-/Set-OabVirtualDirectory, ExternalUrl injection, nltest /dclist, ntds.dit, comsvcs.dll lsass dump, New-MailboxExportRequest to UNC) is lifted directl
